report_name: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Russian-speaking Operator
threat_actors.description: A likely Russian-speaking operator discovered running a sophisticated, AI-assisted phishing campaign targeting Windows users in Mexico with an infostealer. The operator used LLMs to generate phishing content and manage the campaign via a custom admin panel.
threat_actors.goals: To steal credentials and other sensitive information from users in Mexico using an infostealer.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attacker lured victims to a fake government ID-lookup website to deliver an infostealer payload. The phishing content was generated using Large Language Models (LLMs) to make it more convincing.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims were tricked into executing an infostealer payload delivered from a WebDAV share linked from a phishing website.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: An unspecified infostealer was used to harvest credentials from browsers, email clients, and other applications on victims' Windows systems.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used WebDAV, an extension of HTTP, to deliver the infostealer payload to victims. Additionally, stolen data was exfiltrated to the attacker's server over web protocols.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The infostealer payload was transferred to victim machines from an attacker-controlled WebDAV share.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: JavaScript code, believed to be generated by an AI assistant, was found on the attacker's server, likely for use in the phishing website.

tools.name: Simba Service
tools.description: A custom admin panel used by the attacker to track campaign metrics, such as requests and unique IP addresses.

tools.name: Coderrr
tools.description: A possible open-source AI agent used by the attacker to generate JavaScript code for the phishing operation.

tools.name: Large Language Models
tools.description: The attacker used Large Language Models (LLMs) as part of an 'LLM-assisted workflow' to generate convincing phishing content and create detailed test plans for exploits.

tools.name: WebDAV
tools.description: The WebDAV protocol was used as the delivery mechanism for the infostealer payload from the attacker's server.

infrastructure.name: AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit Server
infrastructure.description: An unsecured server that hosted the attacker's entire phishing and malware delivery toolkit, including the Simba Service admin panel and the fake government website.

infrastructure.name: Simba Service Admin Panel
infrastructure.description: A web-based administration panel on the attacker's server used to manage the phishing campaign and track metrics. It was left exposed with default credentials.

infrastructure.name: Fake Mexican Government ID Website
infrastructure.description: A phishing website impersonating a government ID-lookup service, used to lure victims into executing the malware payload.

infrastructure.name: WebDAV Malware Delivery Share
infrastructure.description: A WebDAV share on the attacker's server used to host and deliver the infostealer payload to victims.

indicators.name: Suspicious WebDAV HTTP Methods
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious WebDAV HTTP Methods
id: 2b9a1c3d-f4e5-4b6a-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects HTTP methods (PROPFIND, MKCOL, PUT) characteristic of WebDAV usage, which was used as a malware delivery mechanism in an AI-assisted phishing campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/exposed-server-reveals-ai-assisted.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-21
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.t1071.001
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-method:
      - 'PROPFIND'
      - 'MKCOL'
      - 'PUT'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects HTTP methods characteristic of WebDAV, which was used to deliver malware in this campaign. This may generate false positives in environments where WebDAV is used for legitimate purposes.

indicators.name: Rundll32 Execution from WebDAV Share
indicators.pattern:
title: Rundll32 Execution from WebDAV Share
id: 5e8f9a0b-1c2d-4e3f-a4b5-c6d7e8f9a0b1
status: experimental
description: Detects rundll32.exe executing a DLL from a remote WebDAV/UNC path, a technique observed in an AI-assisted phishing campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/exposed-server-reveals-ai-assisted.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-21
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\\'
      - '.dll'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rundll32.exe with a command line pointing to a remote DLL, likely on a WebDAV or other network share.
